# UC-23.1.1 · Website Conversion Funnel Analysis

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business, Performance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We chart how visitors move from the first page through checkout and where they pause, so we improve the right screens instead of guessing which step loses orders.*

---

## Description

Stages anonymous traffic into an ordered path (for example retail landing → category → product → cart → checkout → confirmation) and measures how many unique visits survive each step using Apache/NGINX combined access logs. The Splunk add-on’s default extractions for `access_combined` and `access_combined_wcookie` make `uri` and `status` immediately searchable; this UC layers bot-aware filtering, query-stripped URI stems, and—when present—a cookie-backed visit key so marketing and product teams can see the sharpest relative drop-off before they fund UX experiments. It explicitly does **not** assert that `clientip` concatenated with `useragent` equals a human; that bootstrap proxy is acceptable for trend lines while you roll out first-party session identifiers from the `cookie` field or from HEC events. The result is a Platform-pillar reporting pattern you can clone for B2B trial funnels or lead-gen forms by swapping the `case()` branches to `/features`, `/signup`, `/thank-you`, and similar.

## Value

Executives and growth leaders use the same funnel to prioritise A/B tests and landing refreshes, because a single high-loss step is visible in one report instead of being inferred from three disconnected spreadsheets. Contrasting the Splunk run with the commerce console or analytics suite explains gaps driven by consent banners, sampling, or payment-gateway redirects while still preserving week-over-week shape inside your own data estate—important when policy forbids exporting raw clickstreams to a SaaS product. Revenue partners can attach an average-order-value lookup per stage for rough dollar-at-risk estimates, and platform teams keep the search under change control because the `case()` map is as much a product contract as a colour token in a design system.

## Implementation

Decide e-commerce, B2B trial, or lead-gen stage labels, then retune the `case()` `match()` patterns to your true route table; ship `access_combined_wcookie` and extract a server-side session from `cookie` before you trust `clientip`+`useragent` alone; add the bot `where` clause and keep `status=200`; for SPAs, add HEC `web:event` rows keyed by `session_id` and `event_name`. Schedule daily or weekly, split panels by UTM (via `referer`/`uri` args) and by host when you have regional stacks. Document the reconciler notes from Step 3 beside the board slide deck so a future leadership change does not relitigate a known analytics delta.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- `index=web` on search heads with roles that list it under **Indexes**; the Apache Web Server add-on (3186) installed on the parsing tier so `access_combined` / `access_combined_wcookie` get field keys without custom `props` beyond your env.
- If you also ingest CDN edge and origin into one index, keep `host=`, `source=`, or a dedicated `index=` token so you can filter panels without mixing two populations accidentally.
- For cookie consent in regulated regions, align with Legal on whether to drop pre-consent page rows at forwarder time; if so, add the agreed `SEDCMD` / `transforms` pattern to the pipeline before searches assume every hit is a person.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
**Path A (primary — Apache/NGINX combined with cookies):** Enable `combined` or `combinedio` `LogFormat` on the origin so Splunk’s TA maps lines to `access_combined` or, preferably, `access_combined_wcookie` when you include `%{Cookie}i` (Apache `LogFormat` ends with a quoted `%{Cookie}i` field). On the forwarder, use the same `inputs.conf` `host` and `sourcetype` names the TA expects, or set `sourcetype=access_combined_wcookie` in a `[monitor://]` stanza that tails `access.log`. Confirm `_raw` events carry `?` and `#` in `uri` so the search can strip the query in SPL.

**Path B (SPA, checkout offloads, and payment redirects):** Emit JSON or key=value lines to HEC with `_time` from the browser or API gateway, including `session_id`, `event_name` (`page_view`, `add_to_cart`, `checkout_started`, `purchase_completed`, …), and `page_path`. Set `sourcetype=web:event` in `inputs.conf` for the HEC token and add `LINE_BREAKER` in `props.conf` if events arrive multiline. That stream becomes authoritative when the SPA only loads `/` and Apache cannot distinguish funnel depth.

**Path C (edge vs origin):** In parallel to `access_combined`, ingest the CDN’s native weblog (Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai) so cache hits and bot scores appear. Tag events with a field `log_tier=edge` vs `log_tier=origin` if both land in the same index; the funnel in this file focuses on `access_combined*` but the troubleshooting section explains how to blend tiers.

**Step 2 — Create the report or alert**
Open **Search** and supply the `spl` field from this use case, adjusting the `index=` / `sourcetype=` tuple to the names you used. **Save As** a report, then optionally **Alert** when `step_delta_pct` for a named stage exceeds a business threshold in the trailing seven days. Keep the time bounds aligned with the Monday leadership review to avoid false alarms on weekend code freezes.

**SPL design notes (read before editing tokens)** The first stanza requires `status=200` so failed navigations, auth challenges, and soft errors do not masquerade as success-path views. The `useragent` filter removes common crawlers, uptime probes, and RUM beacons; tune the `like` list to your own bot roster if your retail vertical attracts niche scrapers. The two `rex` passes create `uri_stem` without the query or fragment, then remove a trailing slash so `/store/` and `/store` coalesce. The `sid_pair` `rex` against `cookie` only fires on `access_combined_wcookie`; for plain `access_combined`, `sid_pair` is null and the `if()` falls back to the fingerprint. The `case()` function maps the stem to ordered labels—replace every `match()` with your sitemap. `stats` uses `dc(visit_key)` to approximate unique funnels, then `streamstats` compares each sorted stage to the previous row; that models sequential drop, not a strict multi-step session graph. For event-level HEC work, copy the `case()` into a new search that uses `event_name` instead of `uri_stem` while keeping the same stage names for dashboard continuity.

**Example `props.conf` fragment for a custom HEC sourcetype** (names illustrative):

```
[web:event]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
CHARSET = UTF-8
```

Pair that with a `transforms` stanza or `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS` if you emit JSON, so `session_id` and `event_name` become first-class fields without fragile `spath`.

**Example Apache `LogFormat` (origin)** — the first line is classic combined; the second is the common pattern that ships cookie material for the `_wcookie` sourcetype.

```
LogFormat "%h %l %u %t \\"%r\\" %>s %b \\"%{Referer}i\\" \\"%{User-agent}i\\"" combined
LogFormat "%h %l %u %t \\"%r\\" %>s %b \\"%{Referer}i\\" \\"%{User-agent}i\\" \\"%{Cookie}i\\"" combinedio
```

**NGINX** users mirror the same idea: append `$http_cookie` to the `log_format` line the reverse proxy writes to the flat file Splunk ingests, then assign `access_combined_wcookie` if field positions still align with the TA, otherwise clone the TA’s `EXTRACT-` and `REPORT-` stanzas under a new sourcetype name and change the `spl` token to match.


**Step 3 — Validate**
Reconcile to Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, or Mixpanel by favouring the **shape** of the funnel (drop-off between neighbouring stages) over raw counts, because packaged tools may sample high-traffic days, apply their own bot lists, and honour cookieless or consent-blocked users. A ±10–20% band on headcount-style metrics is normal; relative curves should be within a few points when the Splunk bot clause is honest. For Shopify, BigCommerce, or Magento, bring in a nightly order extract—CSV or **DB Connect**—and `lookup` by order id or hashed email so gateway redirects that never hit Apache still appear in a companion panel. Document any permanent variance in the runbook (for example internal-only store IDs) so a future auditor does not treat a known gap as a regression.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
Publish a dashboard that pins the report with tokens for `host`, marketing channel, and a rolling seven-day window; wire optional alert actions to marketing operations mailboxes when `step_delta_pct` crosses a band agreed with finance. Pager routes belong to infrastructure alerts—reserve them for the companion HTTP `5xx` use case unless product explicitly wants real-time loss detection. Version the `case()` list beside your route-map wiki page so a routing change in the SPA triggers a search update the same day. If you embed a funnel visual, footnote the session definition (cookie vs fingerprint) in the help text so a new hire does not over-trust the headline integer.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
- **Bot and crawler false positives** — if every stage is unrealistically high, the `useragent` filter is too weak. Tighten the `like` list, add niche scrapers, or—when Cloudflare logs include `cf-bot-management-score` or a vendor equivalent—`rex` the header in the edge sourcetype and restrict rows to scores that your security team classifies as human, `OR` those checks with positive cookie-backed sessions in the origin stream.
- **NAT and proxy session collision** — `visit_key=clientip."::".useragent` conflates carrier-NAT, corporate proxy, or DHCP-reused public IPs. When `dc(clientip)` is tiny versus expected population, or `dc(visit_key)` is enormous versus analytics, move to a cookie `sid_pair` first and audit collisions by sampling `stats count by visit_key` for keys over fifty hits per hour.
- **Single-page app URL collapse** — if every `funnel_step` shows nearly identical `distinct_visitors`, the SPA is serving the same `uri` for all screens; the Apache-only path will not work—switch the dashboard primary panel to the HEC `web:event` search from **Path B** and retire URL stages until events exist.
- **CDN cache vs origin log gap** — a sudden step-down after a CDN deploy with no code change is often a cache-ratio shift. On edge sourcetypes, measure `cf-cache-status` (or the vendor’s equivalent) to see the HIT share; if most HTML is HIT, origin `access_combined` volume is mostly MISS traffic and the funnel will look thin. Present edge and origin in paired panels, or build a lookup-backed field `log_tier` and use `where` in separate saved searches for apples-to-apples storytelling.
- **Time-zone arithmetic** — `_time` is parsed from the server’s combined-log timestamp. If the Apache system clock is UTC but executives read a Pacific board pack, the daily `bin` of funnel counts appears shifted; standardise a `TZ` in the user context or add `| eval day=strftime(_time,"%F")` with an explicit `strptime` offset documented beside the board slide.
- **Query strings and trailing slashes** — the `rex` strip of `?` and `#` and the `uri_stem` `sed` pass prevent `/cart`, `/cart/`, and `/cart?ref=email` from fragmenting a stage; if you add marketing tags without updating the `rex`, reintroduce a `replace` to drop known `utm_*` only when product asks to ignore campaign noise.
- **SEO redirect pairs** — a `302` chain from `/promo` to `/promo-v2` can double-count landing; deduplicate in advanced scenarios with `transaction` on `visit_key` with `maxspan` or by keeping only the first `uri` per visit per minute, accepting that the rule is a policy choice, not a universal truth.
- **Cookie-banner and consent (EU/UK/CH flows)** — when pre-consent hits must not appear in the funnel, your pipeline must `null` or route those events before the index; a sudden jump after a consent-UX relaunch is expected—annotate the chart rather than overfitting thresholds.
- **Non-production host bleed** — confirm production panels include `host=web-prod-*` (or the equivalent) so QA or staging UAT data does not dilute the conversion story; a mistaken `allhosts` macro is the usual root cause.

## SPL

```spl
index=web (sourcetype="access_combined_wcookie" OR sourcetype="access_combined") status=200 earliest=-7d
| eval ua_low=lower(useragent)
| where NOT (like(ua_low,"%googlebot%") OR like(ua_low,"%bingbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%yandexbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%duckduckbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%baiduspider%") OR like(ua_low,"%ahrefsbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%semrushbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%mj12bot%") OR like(ua_low,"%dotbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%petalbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%facebookbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%twitterbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%linkedinbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%slackbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%discordbot%") OR like(ua_low,"%pingdom%") OR like(ua_low,"%uptimerobot%") OR like(ua_low,"%statuscake%") OR like(ua_low,"%newrelic%") OR like(ua_low,"%datadog%") OR match(ua_low, ".*(\\bspider\\b|\\bbot\\b|\\bcrawler\\b|\\bmonitor\\b).*"))
| rex field=uri "^(?<uri_stem>[^?#]+)"
| rex field=uri_stem mode=sed "s|/+$||"
| rex field=cookie max_match=0 "(?i)(?P<sid_pair>(?:JSESSIONID|PHPSESSID|connect\\.sid|__Secure-[^=]+)=[^;\\s]+)"
| eval visit_key=if(isnotnull(sid_pair) AND sid_pair!="", sid_pair, clientip."::".useragent)
| eval funnel_step=case(
    match(uri_stem,"^(/|/home|/index(\\.html)?)$"), "1_Landing",
    match(uri_stem,"^/c(ategory)?/"), "2_Category",
    match(uri_stem,"^/(p(roduct)?/|item/|dp/)"), "3_Product",
    match(uri_stem,"^/cart($|/)"), "4_Cart",
    match(uri_stem,"^/(checkout|payment|billing)($|/)"), "5_Checkout",
    match(uri_stem,"^/(order-confirmation|thank-?you|success|receipt)($|/)"), "6_Confirmation",
    1=1, "0_Other")
| where funnel_step!="0_Other" AND isnotnull(visit_key)
| stats dc(visit_key) as distinct_visitors by funnel_step
| sort funnel_step
| streamstats current=t window=1 last(distinct_visitors) as prior_step_visitors
| eval step_delta_pct=if(funnel_step!="1_Landing" AND isnotnull(prior_step_visitors) AND prior_step_visitors>0, round(100*(prior_step_visitors-distinct_visitors)/prior_step_visitors,1), null())
| table funnel_step, distinct_visitors, prior_step_visitors, step_delta_pct
```

## Visualization

Funnel (ordered bars or Sankey in Dashboard Studio or Classic XML), `splunk.table` of `funnel_step` with `distinct_visitors` and `step_delta_pct`, single value for end-to-end survival from first to last stage, and a line chart of daily `dc(visit_key)` at the confirmation stage.

## Known False Positives

Affiliate and paid-media flight changes can spike early-stage volume without a matching lift at purchase, so the relative drop pattern shifts even when nothing broke on the site. A WAF or bot-management challenge that still returns `status=200` with an interstitial page inflates landing counts while human shoppers never see your catalogue. A/B tests that move inventory between `/c/` and `/p/` URL schemes redistribute counts across the middle stages without a real conversion change. B2B organisations that gate PDF downloads behind the same `uri` as the pricing page make the Product stage look busier than human consideration would imply. A regional promotion that routes EU shoppers through a consent wall may suppress rows at ingest, producing a kink in the line chart that is policy-driven rather than creative failure. A catalog migration that renames every PDP slug in one night invalidates the `case()` list until the search author updates the patterns. Flash crowds from television or influencer drops that exceed session affinity limits can fragment the `visit_key` proxy and depress the apparent funnel width without dropping actual demand. Partner pixels that refire the landing tag when an iframe becomes visible can duplicate early-stage reach without duplicate humans. A sudden TLS or HTTP/2 tuning change on the load balancer alters the mix of `304` vs `200` you choose to count; revalidate the `status=200` guard against your caching policy. Internal pen-test scripts that rotate through the storefront with a browser-like `useragent` can bypass naive bot rules unless you exclude their source IPs. Seasonal job postings that point candidates to a consumer home page can spike Landing without a commerce intent, which product marketing should label as out-of-band noise.

- [Cloudflare Logpush — HTTP requests fields (cache status, bot scores)](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/reference/log-fields/zone/http_requests/)
